page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1.medium is a virtual server instance within the ecs.sn1 family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, alongside 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.sn1.medium demonstrates entry-level performance characteristics. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng registers at 1097.68, placing it in the bottom 25%, while multi-core performance drops to the bottom 10% with a score of 1455.38. Memory bandwidth tests show poor throughput, with read-only small block cached operations at 81.52 GB/sec and uncached large block read and write speeds at 10.27 GB/sec and 9.34 GB/sec respectively. Similarly, Geekbench scores reflect lower-tier processing capabilities, with a single-core score of 913.0 and a multi-core score of 1064.0.

The instance is suited for lightweight, non-resource-intensive workloads. Passmark database operations score at 544.12, and Redis SET operations achieve 1.42 million operations per second. For web serving, the instance delivers a static web serving throughput of 1.25 GB/sec. Small language model inference is possible at 124.56 tokens per second for prompt processing and 36.38 tokens per second for text generation using a 135M model. Given these performance constraints, the ecs.sn1.medium is best utilized for basic utility computing, simple web serving, lightweight background tasks, or development and testing environments where high throughput and low latency are not critical.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn1.medium is an entry-level virtual server instance from the ecs.sn1 family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz, providing 2 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, and 4.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark results place its compute and memory performance consistently in the lower percentiles, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1097.68 and a multi-core score of 1455.38. Memory bandwidth is limited, with uncached write speeds at 9.34 GB/sec. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for low-demand, utility-scale deployments. It is best suited for basic web serving, lightweight background processes, and simple development or testing environments that do not require high-performance compute or memory resources.
